new_value: {"notes":"Investigation findings: 1) Index is populated and healthy after sync (files=55, chunks=545, dirty=false). 2) Direct openclaw memory search returns Slack-history hits for concrete IDs/branches. 3) openclaw agent --agent main returns 'unknown' for memory-only questions, even for known fact ORCH-e2e-029c50 -\u003e ai-orch-56066. 4) Retrieval ranking is query-shape sensitive: query 'what branch was ORCH-e2e-029c50 committed to' returns roadmap chunk as top hit, while 'ORCH-e2e-029c50' or 'ORCH-e2e-029c50 committed' returns slack-history chunks containing answer. Suspected root cause: agent memory tool strategy uses natural-language query without identifier-focused follow-up and likely low/default top-k, so relevant chunks are missed."}
old_value: {"id":"rev-1ml","title":"Fix memory-only retrieval/synthesis gap in openclaw agent main","description":"openclaw agent --agent main with memory-only prompts returns no reliable hits for high-level user-profile questions even after Slack history ingest and successful indexing (files/chunks populated). Investigate retrieval path mismatch between agent/main and CLI memory search, improve query grounding over indexed slack-history corpus, and add verification checks proving memory-only Q/A works for 3 difficult prompts.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"jleechan@example.com","created_at":"2026-03-11T05:19:19Z","created_by":"jleechan","updated_at":"2026-03-11T05:19:19Z"}

new_value: {"notes":"Confirmed root-cause path mismatch: gateway memory tool runs without OPENAI_API_KEY in launchd env. Evidence: (a) session tool results show provider:none/mode:fts-only; (b) gateway logs repeatedly show 'Skipping memory file sync in FTS-only mode (no embedding provider)'; (c) live gateway processes report OPENAI_API_KEY=UNSET; (d) launchd plist at ~/Library/LaunchAgents/ai.openclaw.gateway.plist lacks OPENAI_API_KEY; (e) auth resolver code for provider=openai throws when only openai-codex OAuth exists and OPENAI_API_KEY is absent, causing provider null for memory embeddings. CLI shell commands can still show provider=openai when OPENAI_API_KEY is set in interactive shell, creating the observed mismatch."}

new_value: {"description":"Updated investigation (2026-03-10/11):\\n\\nKey corrections and findings:\\n- Thread context p1773207801 maps to 2026-03-10 22:43:21 PDT (epoch).\\n- In that exact window (~22:42-22:45 local), only one lane-wait event was observed:  at 22:42:53.\\n- The previously cited 28k-78k ms wait cluster (02:48-02:53) is real, but it is a separate time window from p1773207801.\\n\\nPrimary saturation episode (separate severe incident):\\n- Very large main-lane backlog observed around 20:57-20:59 local with queueAhead ~222-227 and waitedMs up to 1,163,431 ms (~19.4m).\\n- Same window shows repeated provider failures and lane task errors (FailoverError rate-limit/auth-profile related) and agent runtime distress.\\n- Node process then hit heap OOM ().\\n\\nInterpretation:\\n- Evidence supports OpenClaw lane saturation + downstream provider/tool instability as root contributors during heavy incidents.\\n- For p1773207801 specifically, available logs do not show an equivalent severe lane backlog at the exact thread timestamp; delay may include model/tool execution latency and noisy plugin/tool config issues rather than pure queue depth.\\n- mctrl  /  files remain orchestration bookkeeping, not OpenClaw lane queue state.\\n"}

{"id":"rev-ljq","title":"Investigate high OpenClaw lane backlog from Slack thread p1773207801","description":"Context:
- User observed very slow OpenClaw responses (~78s+) in Slack thread p1773207801.
- Need distinguish OpenClaw queue backlog from mctrl local queue state.

What I observed (from logs):
- In ~/.openclaw/logs/gateway.err.log, many `lane wait exceeded` events were present.
- Total such events counted: 6404.
- `main` lane dominated (6337 events).
- Peak observed wait: ~1,163,431 ms (~19m).
- Around ~02:48-02:53, repeated `main` lane waits had queueAhead 2-4 and waits ~28k-78k ms.
- One non-main sample: `lane=session:agent:main:main`, wait 3323 ms, queueAhead 0 at 22:42:53.

Correlated noise/errors near same windows:
- FailoverError / API auth and quota-profile issues (no API key / usage limit / no available auth profile).
- tools.profile / tools.allow unknown entry warnings.
- Plugin/toolchain and websocket-token warning activity.

Important distinction discovered:
- mctrl files (`.tracking/bead_session_registry.jsonl`, `.messages/outbox*.jsonl`) are task/session bookkeeping and NOT the OpenClaw lane backlog.
- User earlier correctly noted this mismatch: high OpenClaw delay should not be interpreted as mctrl backlog.

Proposed investigation:
1) Confirm openclaw-config/openclaw.json effective concurrency and subagent limits at time window.
2) Correlate lane waits with API/tooling failures and auth profile selection.
3) Verify why queueAhead is low in some waits while total lane wait is high (retry/failure holding workers).
4) Provide mitigation: worker/thread tuning, failure isolation, alerting thresholds.
","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"jleechan@example.com","created_at":"2026-03-11T06:51:06Z","created_by":"jleechan","updated_at":"2026-03-11T06:51:06Z"}

new_value:
{"description":"Updated investigation (2026-03-10/11):

Key corrections and findings:
- Thread context p1773207801 maps to 2026-03-10 22:43:21 PDT (epoch).
- In that exact window (~22:42-22:45 local), only one lane-wait event was observed: `lane=session:agent:main:main waitedMs=3323 queueAhead=0` at 22:42:53.
- The previously cited 28k-78k ms wait cluster (02:48-02:53) is real, but it is a separate time window from p1773207801.

Primary saturation episode (separate severe incident):
- Very large main-lane backlog observed around 20:57-20:59 local with queueAhead ~222-227 and waitedMs up to 1,163,431 ms (~19.4m).
- Same window shows repeated provider failures and lane task errors (FailoverError rate-limit/auth-profile related) and agent runtime distress.
- Node process then hit heap OOM (`FATAL ERROR: Reached heap limit Allocation failed - JavaScript heap out of memory`).

Interpretation:
- Evidence supports OpenClaw lane saturation + downstream provider/tool instability as root contributors during heavy incidents.
- For p1773207801 specifically, available logs do not show an equivalent severe lane backlog at the exact thread timestamp; delay may include model/tool execution latency and noisy plugin/tool config issues rather than pure queue depth.
- mctrl `.messages` / `.tracking` files remain orchestration bookkeeping, not OpenClaw lane queue state."}

new_value:
{"description":"Scope: immediate triage plan from rev-ljq investigation.

Proposed actions:
1) Fail closed on provider exhaustion
- Ensure primary/fallback model set does not include profiles in long cooldown loops during incidents.
- Add explicit health check gate before dispatching new high-cost runs when provider auth profile is unavailable/rate-limited.

2) Concurrency pressure control (config-first)
- Candidate one-line change for fast mitigation: reduce agents.defaults.maxConcurrent from 4 -> 2 until error rate normalizes.
- Re-evaluate agents.defaults.subagents.maxConcurrent (currently 8); cap lower during incident mode.

3) OOM prevention
- Add runtime memory watchdog/alert and restart policy guard when heap approaches limit.
- Capture heap stats with lane wait diagnostics in same event bundle.

4) Alert thresholds
- Critical alert: lane wait exceeded with waitedMs >= 60000 for main lane, or queueAhead >= 20 sustained for 3+ minutes.
- Major alert: repeated FailoverError rate-limit/auth-profile events > N/min with concurrent lane waits.

5) Noise reduction
- Resolve tools.allow/tools.profile unknown-entry warnings and missing MCP dependencies so diagnostics surface true blockers.

Acceptance criteria:
- Config PR or explicit config diff documented for concurrency mitigation.
- Alert thresholds codified (script/config) and verified against historical log sample.
- Post-change run shows no new waitedMs > 60s in comparable load test window."}
